message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "3 runes + 3 moonstones yield 125 G (120 base + 5 fee, two blocks)"
Current error: Expected 125, received 65 (the hardcoded rune-block branch returns 60 and ignores moonstones)
Passing tests so far: 13

Spec:
- Components (rune, moonstone, ...) each cost 25 G individually
- A block of exactly 3 alike components costs 60 G (instead of 75 G) base premium
- Multiple blocks of different types each get the discount independently
- "Alike" = same type (rune ≠ moonstone)
- Main items (sword, amulet, staff, potion) are NOT components and have their own per-item base premiums (100, 60, 80, 40)

Working directory: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_01-11-17_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking
Run tests with: pnpm test

Refactor the block-handling logic to be general: group component items by type, and for each group of exactly 3 alike apply the block price (60), else sum individually. Main items keep their per-item base.

Current passing tests to preserve:
- empty list → 5
- single sword → 105, amulet → 65, staff → 85, potion → 45
- two swords → 205, sword+amulet → 165
- 1 rune → 30, 2 runes → 55, 3 runes → 65 (block), 4 runes → 105, 7 runes → 180
- 2 runes + 1 moonstone → 80

Implement minimal generalized block logic. Do NOT refactor — just make it work.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current implementation and tests, then make the minimal change to handle the new test case while preserving all passing tests.
Now I'll implement the minimal generalized block logic.

content.text:
All 14 tests pass (including the new "3 runes + 3 moonstones" test).

Green Phase Complete:
- **Implementation**: Generalized the block logic by grouping component items (rune, moonstone) by type, applying the 60 G block price for groups of exactly 3, otherwise summing individually at 25 G each. Main items keep their per-item base premium.
- **Result**: All 14 tests now pass (23 todos remaining).
- **Approach**: Minimal generalization — used a `COMPONENT_TYPES` set to distinguish components from main items, then a count-by-type map with an `if (count === 3)` branch per type. Covers single-block (rune-only) and multi-block (rune+moonstone) cases without anticipating future tests (e.g., 6 runes, 4+ of one component plus 3 of another).

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_01-11-17_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
